what_it_is: Semantic accelerator instruction from the ISA
why_it_exists: Separates what an instruction means from how hardware realizes it.
Makes hardware mapping explicit and analyzable.
Gives every primitive a deterministic hardware home when no fusion matches.
Lets the accelerator declare fused hardware blocks without changing TAIDL semantics.

what_it_is: Bridge from user-facing IP names to calibrated abstraction classes
why_it_exists: Connects semantic realization to measured or modeled hardware costs.

what_it_is: Estimator-facing cost bucket calibrated in primitive_hw_config.json
why_it_exists: Allows the estimator to stay stable even when user-facing IP names change.

what_it_is: Calibrated area/energy database for abstraction classes
why_it_exists: Stores the actual numbers used by the cost model.
